content: Equirectangular (plate carrée) projection maps lon/lat directly to x/y, which is simple but not equal-area or conformal; area/distance distortion scales with cos(latitude) and is small over a local area (e.g., a single TMA) if the cosine correction is applied using the field's reference latitude. For small-area radar-scope-style rendering this local flat-earth approximation (equirectangular with a per-project cos(lat0) x-scale correction, i.e. a local tangent-plane approximation) is standard practice and accurate enough; the failure mode is omitting the cosine correction entirely, which stretches east-west distances and skews bearings/angles on screen relative to true bearings.
